title: Matching: Capital Cities of the World

tasks.instruction: Uzupelnij tabelke, dobierajac okreslenia (A-L) odpowiadajace danej kategorii. Trzy okreslenia podano dodatkowo i nie pasuja one do zadnej kategorii.

options.text: This European capital stands on the River Seine and is famous for the Eiffel Tower.
This European capital lies on the River Tiber and surrounds the tiny state of Vatican City.
This European capital sits on the River Thames, where Big Ben and the Houses of Parliament stand.
This Asian capital is one of the most populous cities in the world and hosts an Imperial Palace.
This Asian capital lies beside the Han River and is the largest city in South Korea.
This Asian capital is home to the Forbidden City and the famous Tiananmen Square.
This is the largest city in the United States, though it is not the national capital today.
This Brazilian city, famous for its Sugarloaf Mountain, is not the country's capital.
This Australian city is home to a famous Opera House and Harbour Bridge, but it is not the country's capital.
The Brandenburg Gate and the remains of a famous wall stand in this German capital.
Red Square and the Kremlin lie at the heart of this Russian capital.
The famous canals and the Anne Frank House can be found in this Dutch capital.

items.id: European capitals standing on a named river
Famous cities that are NOT their country's capital
